summary: exxpress-tool typosquat harvests host info and filesystem data via postinstall
details: Package name 'exxpress-tool' is a typosquat of the widely-used 'express' ecosystem naming. The tarball ships a postinstall.js script that, when npm installs the package, loads 'fs', 'os', and 'http' together and executes code paths that call os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.homedir(), walk the installer's filesystem with fs.existsSync() probes, and transmit results over HTTP. This is a host-reconnaissance + filesystem-enumeration exfiltration pattern running at install time, before the developer has had any opportunity to audit or run the package. The combination of typosquat name + lifecycle-hook-driven sysinfo collection + outbound HTTP channel satisfies the structural fingerprint of an active credential/recon harvester.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
